steps.title: Read the two graph values
steps.content: At $30$ miles per hour, the graph shows a braking distance of about $100$ feet. At $50$ miles per hour, the graph shows a braking distance of about $225$ feet.

steps.title: Subtract the distances
steps.content: The question asks how many feet greater the $50$ mph braking distance is, so subtract: $$225-100=125$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• $75$, is too small.
  • $125$, matches the graph difference. Correct.
  • $175$, subtracts from the wrong value.
  • $250$, is not the difference between the two requested speeds.
That matches the correct answer, B.
